channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: A weekend ritual for Trump's Florida die-hards to get a brief glimpse of their political hero
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The sun was shining outside President Donald Trump's West Palm Beach golf course on Saturday morning when Alan Mentser got a call letting him know that police were shutting down a road nearby. It was almost time to “show the boss a little love.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, received email notices of their mass firing, the latest step in the Trump administration's government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tesla Takedown: Anti-Musk protesters mobilize for Global Day of Protest at hundreds of Tesla locations
item.description: A Global Day of Protest has been planned for Saturday, during which anti-Musk protesters plan to demonstrate at hundreds of Tesla showroom locations across the globe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Richard Carlbom ascends to top role at Minnesota DFL Party as its first new chair in 14 years
item.description: Richard Carlbom was chosen as the Minnesota DFL Party chair, rising to the top post after years of working behind the scenes on party elections and causes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump releases powerful video highlighting US-Greenland bond after deadly Nazi attack
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a powerful video Friday highlighting the strong historical bonds between Greenland and the United States amid ongoing efforts to annex the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top vaccine official allegedly pushed out of FDA: report
item.description: Dr. Peter Marks, who played a key role in Operation Warp Speed, was reportedly forced out of the FDA, according to the Wall Street Journal. In his purported resignation letter, Marks slams HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: High-profile ICE arrests of Tufts, Alabama students spotlight growing Trump sweep
item.description: Headline-grabbing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) arrests this week at Tufts University and the University of Alabama show growing momentum behind the Trump administration's battle against pro-Palestinian foreign students and faculty.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota food shelves worried as USDA cancels truckloads of food
item.description: This week, the U.S. Department of Agriculture canceled almost half of food shelf orders from Minnesota farmers under the federal Commodity Credit Corporation. Organizations providing nutrition programs say they are concerned about how to provide for their clientele.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Musk says 'civilization will disappear' unless birth rates change
item.description: "The birth rate is very low in almost every country, and unless that changes, civilization will disappear," Musk said in an interview with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey's GOP primary for governor could pivot on Trump, a part-time resident
item.description: The most important Republican in New Jersey’s race for governor this year might well be a part-time resident who burnished his reputation and his brand near the Atlantic City Boardwalk.
item.dc:creator: Mike Catalini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why Trump's push for frigid Greenland is about icing out US adversaries
item.description: President Donald Trump’s push to acquire Greenland highlights the island’s growing strategic value, from rare earth mineral wealth to its role in Arctic defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominates former Arizona attorney general for US ambassador to Serbia
item.description: President Donald Trump has nominated former Arizona Attorney General Mark Brnovich to serve in his administration as the next U.S. ambassador to Serbia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department sends Congress letter saying it's taking over USAID
item.description: A source tells NewsNation a team trying to organize support for victims of the Myanmar earthquake was told to leave the office at midday.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: The Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party will elect a new chair on Saturday
item.description: Two Democrats are vying to lead the state’s DFL Party. Members of the DFL will vote Saturday for a replacement to longtime party chair Ken Martin who left earlier this year to head up the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration investigates Maine for claims of withholding gender transitions from parents
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The administration of President Donald Trump is investigating Maine with a claim that dozens of school districts in the state violate federal law by withholding information about students' gender transitioning from parents. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota loses $27.5 million in behavioral health funds as part of Trump administration cuts
item.description: Overnight this week, the Minnesota Department of Human Services lost hundreds of contracts, amounting to $27.5 million, after massive federal cuts from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Gretchen Brown, MPR News

item.title: Major law firm reaches deal with Trump to avoid White House order even as two other firms sue
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prominent international law firm reached a deal with President Donald Trump on Friday to dedicate at least $100 million in free legal services and to review its hiring practices, averting a punishing executive order like the ones directed at nearly a half-dozen other major legal
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Republicans say Sigal Chattah nominated for Nevada U.S. Attorney post
item.description: Nevada Republicans are saying Sigal Chattah has been nominated to serve as interim U.S. Attorney overseeing Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Countries that have issued travel advisories for US
item.description: Denmark, Finland, France, and Germany noted the U.S. requires individuals entering to choose male or female on ESTA and visa applications.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear displays his colors in veto fights with Republican lawmakers
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Knowing the odds of winning were stacked against him, Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear nevertheless vetoed bills sent his way by the Republican-dominated Kentucky legislature with by-now familiar themes: abortion, Medicaid and diversity initiatives.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DFLers at Minnesota Capitol rip Musk’s ideas while the GOP wants a closer look
item.description: Billionaire and top Trump adviser Elon Musk is reshaping the federal government. In Minnesota, Democrats are increasingly turning Musk mentions into a punching bag. And Republicans say his work could be replicated in St. Paul.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Musk promises to go to Wisconsin to personally deliver $2 million to voters in Supreme Court race
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk says he plans to hold a rally in Wisconsin to “personally hand over” $2 million to a pair of voters who have already cast their ballots in the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DOJ investigating California universities over affirmative action
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Thursday it will be investigating multiple California universities to ensure they are in compliance with a Supreme Court ruling banning affirmative action in admissions.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota doctor: Hospitals in ‘existential crisis’ and potential Medicaid cuts won’t help
item.description: Dr. Rahul Koranne, president and CEO of the Minnesota Hospital Association, joined Cathy Wurzer to talk about what recent job loss and federal volatility mean for the health care of all Minnesotans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Per-pupil funding going up $2 next year unless something changes in Nevada budget
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's proposed state budget sets per-pupil funding in 2026 at $9,416, just $2 higher than the current funding level. Going higher than that is "impracticable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California Assembly Democrats announce plans to leave X over disinformation, hate speech
item.description: California Assembly Democrats announced plans to leave X over disinformation, lack of content moderation and hate speech.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: GOP leaders accused of making threats to block bill to let new moms vote remotely
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties teamed up to force a House vote on a measure allowing new parents to vote by proxy for 12 weeks, but House Speaker Mike Johnson opposes it on Constitutional grounds.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, and Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, PIX 11

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Florida House proposes largest sales tax cut in state history
item.description: The Florida House of Representatives is proposing the largest cut to sales tax in state history, from 6% to 5.25%.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC mayoral poll: Cuomo grows lead, Mamdani gains traction
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is still holding onto the lead in the New York City mayoral race – but one other candidate is also gaining support, according to new exclusive polling conducted by PIX11 News in partnership with Emerson College and The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump will announce auto tariffs at a White House news conference
item.description: President Donald Trump will announce tariffs on auto imports, a move the White Houses claims would foster domestic manufacturing that could also put a financial squeeze on automakers that depend on global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court seems divided over Louisiana case of congressional map with 2 Black majority seats
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed closely divided Monday over a challenge to Louisiana’s congressional map, which added a second Black majority district.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, Fox 5
